jujutsuCodes.title: Jujutsu Randomizer Codes
jujutsuCodes.intro: Redeem the latest Jujutsu Randomizer codes for Yen, then spend it on cosmetics such as skins and titles. Codes are usually posted through the Roblox experience description, Dope Interactive community channels, or update announcements.

requirements.title: Join the Roblox community
requirements.detail: Codes require the player account to be in the Dope Interactive Roblox community.

requirements.title: Like the Roblox experience
requirements.detail: Like the Jujutsu Randomizer experience page before launching a fresh session.

requirements.title: Use exact spelling
requirements.detail: Codes are case-sensitive and should be pasted exactly as written.

redeemSteps.title: Open the Roblox experience page
redeemSteps.body: Join the Dope Interactive community from the creator link, then like Jujutsu Randomizer on Roblox.

redeemSteps.title: Launch a fresh server
redeemSteps.body: Start Jujutsu Randomizer after the join and like checks are complete.

redeemSteps.title: Open the Code menu
redeemSteps.body: Press the Code button on the left side of the in-game screen.

redeemSteps.title: Paste the code
redeemSteps.body: Enter one working code into the Insert Codes box. The reward is claimed once the game accepts the code.

redeemSteps.title: Repeat for each code
redeemSteps.body: Redeem the newest codes first, because update and milestone codes tend to rotate out when new ones arrive.

jujutsuCodes.expiredNote: Older Jujutsu Randomizer codes rotate out as new updates arrive. If a code no longer accepts, it has been retired and replaced by the newest active set.

jujutsuBeginnerGuide.title: Jujutsu Randomizer Beginner Guide
jujutsuBeginnerGuide.intro: A fast start guide for players who just spawned into the arena. The core loop is simple: adapt to random skills, survive rounds, win fights, earn Yen, and unlock cosmetics.

steps.title: Prepare your account before entering
steps.body: Join the Dope Interactive Roblox community and like the Roblox experience. This unlocks code redemption checks and prevents valid codes from failing.
steps.tip: Redeem active Yen codes before grinding matches.

steps.title: Spawn, then read your random kit
steps.body: Each round can hand you a random character, random skills, and sometimes weapon or ability tools. Check whether your current kit has burst damage, movement, a counter, a domain, or healing before rushing the nearest player.
steps.tip: Classify your skills first; attack second.

steps.title: Use movement to stay alive
steps.body: Mobility skills such as Aqua Burst, 24 FPS Dash, Surprise Attack, and Boogie Woogie are valuable because surviving longer usually means more chances to earn Yen and win the round.
steps.tip: Escape bad fights instead of trading every hit.

steps.title: Look for easy starter combos
steps.body: Simple control into burst is the easiest beginner pattern. Frame Punch can freeze an enemy briefly, then follow with Hollow Purple, Angry Barrage, Dismantle, Furnace, or another high-damage skill while they are stuck.
steps.tip: Use one stun or control tool before your biggest damage move.

steps.title: Save domains for crowded fights
steps.body: Domain skills are strongest when several enemies are close together. Infinite Void, Malevolent Shrine, Shadow Garden, and Time Cell Moon Palace can control space, punish groups, and change a round quickly.
steps.tip: Do not waste a domain on one target that can simply run away.

steps.title: Win or survive rounds for Yen
steps.body: Winning rounds gives Yen, and Yen is used to unlock skins or titles. The game rewards adapting to whatever kit you roll rather than buying a permanent combat build.
steps.tip: Treat early Yen as cosmetic currency, not a power shortcut.

steps.title: Spend early resources carefully
steps.body: Use codes first, then play rounds for more Yen. Since skills are randomized by the game loop, spend early Yen on skins or titles only after you know what you actually want to display.
steps.tip: Redeem codes first, grind second, buy cosmetics last.

steps.title: Learn the danger signs
steps.body: Blood skills such as Piercing Blood and Charged Piercing Blood cost HP, while Black Hole only works at low HP and eliminates the user after activation. These are strong tools, but beginners should watch health closely before pressing them.
steps.tip: Do not sacrifice HP unless the trade wins the fight.

jujutsuTierList.title: Jujutsu Randomizer Tier List
jujutsuTierList.intro: Use this tier list to recognize strong rolls quickly. S-tier skills can swing a round by themselves, A-tier skills are consistent fight winners, B-tier skills are useful but more situational, and C-tier covers basic weapons or low-damage tools that need stronger support.

entries.name: Hollow Purple
entries.reason: 35 damage after charging with massive knockback.

entries.name: Furnace
entries.reason: 19 damage fire explosion with knockback and strong multi-target value.

entries.name: Domain Expansion: Infinite Void
entries.reason: Lets the user run through enemies, damaging and knocking them back for several seconds.

entries.name: Domain Expansion: Malevolent Shrine
entries.reason: Summons a shrine that deals 24 damage across a huge area.

entries.name: Time Cell Moon Palace
entries.reason: Immobilizes enemies inside the domain every two seconds and adds damage pressure.

entries.name: Angry Barrage
entries.reason: 18 damage punch sequence that stuns while hitting.

entries.name: Lucky Uppercut
entries.reason: 16 damage punch with a fast cooldown.

entries.name: Dismantle
entries.reason: 14 damage with knockback, useful for resetting space.

entries.name: Instant Zap
entries.reason: Teleports to the enemy and deals 14 damage.

entries.name: Surprise Attack
entries.reason: Punches the enemy, teleports behind them, and deals 15 damage.

entries.name: Black Flash
entries.reason: 15 damage with a short cooldown; pairs well with the Vessel passive.

entries.name: Explode!
entries.reason: Deals 15 damage to nearby enemies.

entries.name: Aqua Burst
entries.reason: Launches the user forward and deals 12 damage.

entries.name: Hydro Strike
entries.reason: A dependable filler attack when stronger skills are on cooldown.

entries.name: Basic weapons
entries.reason: Lower practical impact unless boosted by a weapon-focused character passive.

entries.name: Low-damage abilities
entries.reason: Need setup, positioning, or stronger companion skills to matter in PvP.

entries.name: Big Brother
entries.reason: Blood-based skills no longer consume HP and deal 20% more damage.

entries.name: Vengeful Girl
entries.reason: All weapons deal 20% more damage.

entries.name: Adapt Guy
entries.reason: Damage taken inside domains is reduced by three times.

entries.name: Vessel
entries.reason: Black Flash abilities deal 20% more damage.

entries.name: Besto Friendo
entries.reason: Starts with +15 HP for a safer health buffer.

jujutsuAbilities.title: Jujutsu Randomizer Abilities
jujutsuAbilities.intro: Abilities in Jujutsu Randomizer fall into damage, mobility, defensive, domain, control, and risky self-cost categories. Strong play comes from matching the skill to the situation: stun before burst, save domains for groups, use movement to escape bad trades, and watch HP before using blood or sacrifice skills.

abilities.name: Hollow Purple
abilities.effect: Charges, then deals 35 damage with massive knockback.
abilities.combo: Frame Punch into Hollow Purple; use Boogie Woogie or Surprise Attack to create a better angle first.
abilities.counter: Keep moving, avoid lining up during the charge, and punish the user if the shot misses.

abilities.name: Furnace
abilities.effect: Creates a fire explosion that deals 19 damage and pushes enemies away.
abilities.combo: Use after a mobility engage or when enemies are slowed inside a domain.
abilities.counter: Do not chase directly into close range; bait the explosion, then re-engage.

abilities.name: Angry Barrage
abilities.effect: A rapid punch combo that stuns enemies while dealing 18 damage.
abilities.combo: Frame Punch into Angry Barrage, then follow with a quick finisher such as Black Flash or Dismantle.
abilities.counter: Do not trade in point-blank range; use movement, counters, or knockback before the barrage starts.

abilities.name: Frame Punch
abilities.effect: Deals 5 damage and freezes the enemy for 2 seconds.
abilities.combo: Frame Punch into Hollow Purple, Angry Barrage, Furnace, or Dismantle.
abilities.counter: Respect its setup value; dodge the opener instead of trying to out-trade the follow-up.

abilities.name: Instant Zap
abilities.effect: Teleports to an enemy and deals 14 damage.
abilities.combo: Instant Zap into Lucky Uppercut, Black Flash, or Dismantle.
abilities.counter: Hold a counter, area hit, or knockback option when the user is looking for a teleport engage.

abilities.name: Surprise Attack
abilities.effect: Punches the enemy, teleports behind them, and deals 15 damage.
abilities.combo: Surprise Attack into Lucky Uppercut, Dismantle, or Black Flash.
abilities.counter: Expect the behind-the-back placement and turn quickly with an area or counter option.

abilities.name: Boogie Woogie
abilities.effect: Switches positions with a nearby player.
abilities.combo: Swap a target into Furnace, Malevolent Shrine pressure, or another area threat.
abilities.counter: Keep spacing wide and avoid standing near hazards or enemy teammates.

abilities.name: Domain Expansion: Infinite Void
abilities.effect: Lets the user run through enemies, dealing damage and knockback for several seconds.
abilities.combo: Activate when enemies are already committed to a brawl, then chain knockback into burst damage.
abilities.counter: Avoid tight clusters and use defensive tools or movement to leave the danger zone.

abilities.name: Domain Expansion: Malevolent Shrine
abilities.effect: Summons a shrine that deals 24 damage across a huge area.
abilities.combo: Use after a position swap, freeze, or crowded chase to force multiple enemies into damage.
abilities.counter: Leave the area immediately and do not fight inside the shrine unless protected.

abilities.name: Simple Domain
abilities.combo: Use defensively, then counterattack with your highest-value move when the enemy commits too early.
abilities.counter: Do not waste your strongest attack into the invulnerability window; wait it out.

abilities.name: Reverse Cursed Technique
abilities.combo: Use after disengaging with Aqua Burst, 24 FPS Dash, or Boogie Woogie.
abilities.counter: Pressure the user before they can reset the fight and regain safe spacing.

abilities.name: Black Hole
abilities.effect: Only activates below 20 HP; eliminates nearby enemies and then eliminates the user.
abilities.combo: Pair with position manipulation like Boogie Woogie to drag enemies into the danger zone.
abilities.counter: Do not crowd a low-HP Black Hole user; back away and force them to waste the condition.

jujutsuCharacters.title: Jujutsu Randomizer Characters
jujutsuCharacters.intro: Jujutsu Randomizer sends your Roblox avatar into PvP rounds with randomized Jujutsu Kaisen-inspired skills, then uses Yen progression to unlock skins and titles. The practical rule is simple: search by character theme to find the look, but do not treat a skin as a fixed moveset.

characters.name: Random-Skill Arena Fighter
characters.source: Default avatar plus any equipped skin
characters.appearance: Your Roblox avatar enters the arena and receives a random set of Jujutsu Kaisen-inspired skills for the round.
characters.detail: The moveset changes through the random skill system; the equipped look does not lock a permanent character class.

characters.name: Gojo / The Strongest Theme
characters.source: Character and skin references plus official promotional material
characters.appearance: White-haired, strongest-themed Jujutsu Kaisen styling used in character and promotional material.
characters.detail: Use this entry for Gojo searches, strongest-themed looks, and blue or energy-style skill associations; the actual round skills are still randomized.
characters.terms: Gojo, Satoru Gojo, The Strongest, Limitless

characters.name: Sukuna / King of Curse Theme
characters.source: Character and skin references plus historical code naming such as KINGOFCURSE and VIPFORSUKUNAHEHE
characters.appearance: Sukuna-inspired curse-king styling tied to red, marked, and king-of-curses search intent.
characters.detail: Use this entry for Sukuna searches and curse-themed cosmetics; skill pulls remain round-based instead of skin-based.
characters.terms: Sukuna, King of Curse, King of Curses, VIP

characters.name: Panda Skin
characters.appearance: A panda or bear outfit used as a recognizable early cosmetic target.
characters.detail: Cosmetic-only appearance change; it does not change random skills, damage, or round rewards.
characters.terms: Panda, panda skin, cheap skin, starter skin

characters.name: Character Image Roster
characters.appearance: A visual roster used for browsing character looks, skin inspiration, and character-name searches.
characters.detail: Use the roster to identify looks, then confirm unlocks and prices inside the in-game shop.

jujutsuYenGuide.title: Jujutsu Randomizer Yen Guide
jujutsuYenGuide.intro: Yen is the main progression currency for skins, titles, and finishers. New players should claim code Yen first, buy one useful low-cost cosmetic, then save for titles or finishers. The core loop is direct: win rounds to earn more Yen, then use it to unlock cosmetics.

rows.how: Join Dope Interactive, like the Roblox experience, open the game, press the Code button, and enter the code exactly.
rows.use: Starter Yen for skins, titles, or finishers

rows.how: Use the in-game Code button after the group and like requirements are complete.
Enter the code from the official game-page code banner in the in-game code box.
rows.use: Starts shopping without waiting on several rounds

rows.how: Enter the arena, survive the random-skill chaos, and win the round.
rows.use: Main repeatable Yen source after codes

rows.how: Stay alive while other players are eliminated; stronger placement improves the payout path.
rows.use: Consistent Yen when wins are not happening yet

rows.how: Buy from the skin or shop flow with Yen.
rows.use: Change your look without changing randomized skills

rows.how: Unlock or buy through the title or shop flow with Yen.
Buy through the finisher or shop flow with Yen.

jujutsuSkinsTitles.title: Jujutsu Randomizer Skins And Titles
jujutsuSkinsTitles.intro: Skins, titles, and finishers are Yen sinks tied to collection and style. They make your character easier to recognize and more fun to show off, but the random skill system still decides what tools you fight with during each round.

cosmetics.name: Skins
cosmetics.unlock: Buy from the Skins or Shop flow with Yen earned from codes and rounds.
cosmetics.effect: Changes the visible character look in the lobby and arena.
cosmetics.relation: Cosmetic appearance only; skins do not replace the random skill system.

cosmetics.name: Panda Skin
cosmetics.unlock: Buy from the skin shop with Yen at an entry-level price tier.
cosmetics.effect: Equips a panda or bear-style outfit for a recognizable beginner look.
cosmetics.relation: A beginner-friendly cosmetic target that gives new players a recognizable look without changing their combat tools.

cosmetics.name: Titles
cosmetics.unlock: Unlock or buy through the Titles area using Yen progression.
cosmetics.effect: Shows a title-style label for identity, status, or collection progress.
cosmetics.relation: Display-focused; titles are a flex item rather than a direct combat upgrade.

cosmetics.name: Finishers
cosmetics.unlock: Buy through the Finishers area with Yen.
cosmetics.effect: Changes the finishing move or execution effect used after a takedown.
cosmetics.relation: Adds flair to eliminations and gives players another reason to grind Yen after buying skins.

cosmetics.name: Black Flash Finisher
cosmetics.unlock: Acquire through the finisher unlock path; shop price varies by listing.
cosmetics.effect: Adds a Black Flash-style finishing hit effect to your eliminations.
cosmetics.relation: Used as a finisher-style effect rather than a fixed starter moveset.

cosmetics.name: Code-Funded Cosmetics
cosmetics.unlock: Redeem active codes, then spend the Yen in the cosmetic shop.
cosmetics.effect: Turns code rewards into skins, titles, or finishers before regular win rewards take over.
cosmetics.relation: The fastest early cosmetic route before regular win rewards take over.

jujutsuTrelloUpdates.title: Jujutsu Randomizer Trello And Updates

The fastest update-check order is Roblox game page first, Discord announcements second, Trello references third, and the in-game shop last for final prices.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Jujutsu Randomizer

questions.question: What is Jujutsu Randomizer?
questions.answer: Jujutsu Randomizer is a Roblox PvP arena fighter where every round rolls a random set of Jujutsu Kaisen-inspired skills. You win fights, earn Yen, and unlock skins, titles, and finishers.

questions.question: Where do I redeem Jujutsu Randomizer codes?
questions.answer: Join the Dope Interactive Roblox community, like the Jujutsu Randomizer experience page, launch a fresh server, press the Code button on the left of the in-game screen, and paste a code into the Insert Codes box.

questions.question: What are the active Jujutsu Randomizer codes right now?
questions.answer: The current public Jujutsu Randomizer code set from the official game-page banner is FINNALYANUPDATE for 175 Yen, AURAMONSTER for 125 Yen, and 8MVISITS for 125 Yen. Codes are case-sensitive and single-use per Roblox account.

questions.question: How do I get more Yen in Jujutsu Randomizer?
questions.answer: Redeem active Jujutsu Randomizer codes first for a starter fund, then win rounds and survive longer for repeatable Yen. Spend early Yen on one low-cost skin, then save for titles or finishers.

questions.question: Do skins change my skills in Jujutsu Randomizer?
questions.answer: No. Skins, titles, and finishers are cosmetic only. Jujutsu Randomizer hands you a new random skill kit each round, so your look never locks a permanent moveset.

questions.question: Where can I play Jujutsu Randomizer?
questions.answer: Jujutsu Randomizer is available on Roblox through the official experience page by Dope Interactive. It is free to play inside the Roblox platform.

cta.title: Ready to Roll into Jujutsu Randomizer?
cta.description: Join the Jujutsu Randomizer community on Discord for codes, updates, skill rolls, and arena discussions!

codes.title: Jujutsu Randomizer Codes
codes.description: Latest working Jujutsu Randomizer codes for free Yen, redemption steps, reward details, and the expired code list.
codes.metaTitle: Jujutsu Randomizer Codes - Free Yen & Rewards
codes.metaDescription: Redeem active Jujutsu Randomizer codes for free Yen, follow the redemption steps, and track which codes have expired.
codes.whatIsDescription: Jujutsu Randomizer codes are free reward strings released through the Roblox experience description, Dope Interactive community channels, and update posts. Redeeming them grants Yen you can spend on skins, titles, and cosmetics.

whySteps.step1Description: Claim active codes before queuing into matches so you start with currency to spend.
whySteps.step2Description: Turn code Yen into skins, titles, and finishers without grinding early fights.
whySteps.step3Description: New update and milestone codes rotate in regularly, so check back after each patch.

guide.title: Jujutsu Randomizer Guides
guide.description: Walkthroughs for Jujutsu Randomizer mechanics, secrets, egg hunts, sealed box locations, map names, and signature techniques.
guide.metaDescription: Master Jujutsu Randomizer with guides on QTE, sealed boxes, egg locations, map names, code redemption, and signature techniques.
guide.whatIsDescription: Jujutsu Randomizer guides explain how to navigate the arena, trigger QTE, find hidden eggs and sealed boxes, and use signature techniques like Deadly Sentencing. Each guide walks through one mechanic or secret with clear steps.

whySteps.step1Description: Understand random skill rolls, QTE timing, and how matches are won before you queue in.
whySteps.step2Description: Locate sealed boxes, hidden eggs, and named maps that unlock new rewards and areas.
whySteps.step3Description: Apply signature abilities like Deadly Sentencing with the right timing and combo flow.

characters.title: Jujutsu Randomizer Characters
characters.description: Every Jujutsu Randomizer character theme — Gojo, Sukuna, Yuta, Hakari, Mahoraga, and more — with passives, skill pools, and skin sources.
characters.metaDescription: Browse all Jujutsu Randomizer character themes including Gojo, Sukuna, Yuta, Hakari, and Mahoraga with passive traits and unlock sources.
characters.whatIsDescription: Jujutsu Randomizer characters are themed loadouts inspired by Jujutsu Kaisen — Gojo, Sukuna, Yuta, Hakari, Mahoraga, Kenjaku, and more. Each character theme pairs with specific skill pools, passive traits, and cosmetic sources.

whySteps.step1Description: Pick a character theme whose passive and skill pool fits aggressive, defensive, or counter play.
whySteps.step2Description: See which characters are tied to eggs, sealed boxes, or milestones so you can target them.
whySteps.step3Description: Find which character themes come with exclusive skins and where to unlock them.

abilities.title: Jujutsu Randomizer Abilities
abilities.description: Every Jujutsu Randomizer ability and move — Limitless Unsealed, Black Hole, Hollow Nuke, Adapt — with effects, combos, and counters.
abilities.metaDescription: Learn every Jujutsu Randomizer ability including Limitless Unsealed, Black Hole, Hollow Nuke, and Adapt with effects, combos, and counters.
abilities.whatIsDescription: Jujutsu Randomizer abilities are the random skills rolled each round — Limitless Unsealed, Black Hole, Hollow Nuke, Adapt, and more. They define your offense, defense, and domain tools for that match.

whySteps.step1Description: Chain abilities like Hollow Nuke and Black Hole for burst damage and zone control.
whySteps.step2Description: Recognize which abilities counter yours so you can swap tactics mid-fight.
whySteps.step3Description: Since skills randomize each round, knowing every ability keeps you effective no matter what you roll.

items.title: Jujutsu Randomizer Items
items.description: Every Jujutsu Randomizer item — sealed box, Sukuna finger, cursed fingers, Zenin treasure, eggs — with locations, uses, and rewards.
items.metaDescription: Find every Jujutsu Randomizer item including sealed boxes, Sukuna fingers, cursed fingers, Zenin treasure, and eggs with locations and rewards.
items.whatIsDescription: Jujutsu Randomizer items are collectibles and consumables hidden across the map — sealed boxes, Sukuna fingers, cursed fingers, Zenin treasure, and eggs. Each one unlocks rewards, lore, or cosmetic upgrades.

whySteps.step1Description: Open sealed boxes and treasure caches for Yen, skins, and rare upgrades.
whySteps.step2Description: Gather cursed fingers and eggs to finish character-themed collection tracks.
whySteps.step3Description: Track down hidden eggs and Zenin treasure that most players walk straight past.

skins.title: Jujutsu Randomizer Skins
skins.description: Every Jujutsu Randomizer skin — best skins, unlock conditions, and cosmetic sources for every character theme.
skins.metaDescription: Browse Jujutsu Randomizer skins, find the best skins, and learn the unlock conditions and sources for every cosmetic.
skins.whatIsDescription: Jujutsu Randomizer skins are cosmetic overrides for your character theme — earned through Yen, codes, eggs, and milestone unlocks. They change your look without affecting skill rolls or stats.

whySteps.step1Description: Equip rare skins to look distinct during matches, finishers, and update events.
whySteps.step2Description: Turn Yen from codes and wins into the best-value skins first.
whySteps.step3Description: Pair skins with their matching character theme for a full cosmetic loadout.
